pack_purpose: Deterministic narrow follow-up pack for targeted escalation. It defines the crux questions and operational budgets, while source choice may remain model-driven within runtime-enforced tool guardrails.

supporting_persona_summaries.main_thesis: The market is directionally right that BTC is likely to finish above 70000 on Apr 20, but the 0.87 price looks mildly rich relative to remaining five-day volatility into one exact Binance minute close.
main_logical_chain: Start from the market's 0.87 price as an information-rich prior.
Verify the governing source and confirm BTC is currently around 74000 on Binance.
Check one independent contextual source and confirm the broader market also sees BTC around 74000.
Apply a modest discount because the contract settles on one future minute close several days away.
strongest_supports: Binance BTCUSDT traded around 73994.6 during the run, well above 70000.
CoinGecko independently showed BTC around 74006 with the sampled 1-day range above 70000.
strongest_disconfirmers: Settlement depends on one future Binance 12:00 ET minute close, not today's spot.
BTC can move more than 5% over five days, so a sub-70000 close remains plausible.
unresolved_ambiguities: How stable the current 74000 regime remains through Apr 20 noon ET.
Whether any near-term catalyst or shock emerges before settlement.
supporting_persona_summaries.what_would_change_view: A sustained Binance move back toward or below 70000, or new evidence of exchange-specific/resolution-surface issues, would push the estimate lower.
supporting_persona_summaries.timing_relevance: High: the market resolves at an exact future minute on 2026-04-20 12:00 ET, so remaining path volatility matters.
supporting_persona_summaries.source_quality_view: High-quality direct governing-source check plus one medium-high-quality contextual verification; source-of-truth ambiguity is fairly low but not zero.
